Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and B is [AB] cM (AB)</li><li>The distance between genes A and H is [AH] cM (AH)</li><li>The distance between genes B and H is [BH] cM (BH)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AB	26		AH	8		BH	20		geneorder	ahb	bha
FIB_PLUS	<p>bb59_644b</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene N</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene P</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene T</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes N and P is [NP] cM (NP)</li><li>The distance between genes N and T is [NT] cM (NT)</li><li>The distance between genes P and T is [PT] cM (PT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	NP	35		NT	18		PT	25		geneorder	ntp	ptn
FIB_PLUS	<p>d695_3ae7</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene F</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene P</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and F is [AF] cM (AF)</li><li>The distance between genes A and P is [AP] cM (AP)</li><li>The distance between genes F and P is [FP] cM (FP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AF	18		AP	5		FP	14		geneorder	apf	fpa
FIB_PLUS	<p>505b_ccb2</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene D</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene E</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">d</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and E is [DE] cM (DE)</li><li>The distance between genes D and K is [DK] cM (DK)</li><li>The distance between genes E and K is [EK] cM (EK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DE	15		DK	35		EK	25		geneorder	dek	ked

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene J</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and J is [FJ] cM (FJ)</li><li>The distance between genes F and T is [FT] cM (FT)</li><li>The distance between genes J and T is [JT] cM (JT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FJ	32		FT	12		JT	25		geneorder	ftj	jtf

FIB_PLUS	<p>fa3f_c3ed</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene M</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene R</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes M and R is [MR] cM (MR)</li><li>The distance between genes M and Y is [MY] cM (MY)</li><li>The distance between genes R and Y is [RY] cM (RY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	MR	16		MY	25		RY	36		geneorder	rmy	ymr
FIB_PLUS	<p>88e5_faed</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene J</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene M</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and J is [EJ] cM (EJ)</li><li>The distance between genes E and M is [EM] cM (EM)</li><li>The distance between genes J and M is [JM] cM (JM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EJ	9		EM	32		JM	25		geneorder	ejm	mje
FIB_PLUS	<p>aea2_a526</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and H is [AH] cM (AH)</li><li>The distance between genes A and Y is [AY] cM (AY)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AH	36		AY	15		HY	24		geneorder	ayh	hya
FIB_PLUS	<p>b9cc_bc9f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and K is [AK] cM (AK)</li><li>The distance between genes A and T is [AT] cM (AT)</li><li>The distance between genes K and T is [KT] cM (KT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AK	38		AT	16		KT	25		geneorder	atk	kta
FIB_PLUS	<p>153b_1048</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene H</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">a</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and H is [AH] cM (AH)</li><li>The distance between genes A and X is [AX] cM (AX)</li><li>The distance between genes H and X is [HX] cM (HX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AH	5		AX	28		HX	25		geneorder	ahx	xha

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene J</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene W</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and J is [EJ] cM (EJ)</li><li>The distance between genes E and W is [EW] cM (EW)</li><li>The distance between genes J and W is [JW] cM (JW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EJ	11		EW	32		JW	25		geneorder	ejw	wje

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene F</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene K</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene N</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and K is [FK] cM (FK)</li><li>The distance between genes F and N is [FN] cM (FN)</li><li>The distance between genes K and N is [KN] cM (KN)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FK	10		FN	24		KN	32		geneorder	kfn	nfk
FIB_PLUS	<p>63d8_779d</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene B</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene P</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene R</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and P is [BP] cM (BP)</li><li>The distance between genes B and R is [BR] cM (BR)</li><li>The distance between genes P and R is [PR] cM (PR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BP	27		BR	3		PR	25		geneorder	brp	prb
FIB_PLUS	<p>8e8b_29c4</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene B</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene K</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene P</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and K is [BK] cM (BK)</li><li>The distance between genes B and P is [BP] cM (BP)</li><li>The distance between genes K and P is [KP] cM (KP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BK	33		BP	15		KP	20		geneorder	bpk	kpb
FIB_PLUS	<p>04ac_88c1</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">b</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and C is [BC] cM (BC)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BC	32		BX	10		CX	25		geneorder	bxc	cxb
FIB_PLUS	<p>9fbb_a085</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">c</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and J is [CJ] cM (CJ)</li><li>The distance between genes C and T is [CT] cM (CT)</li><li>The distance between genes J and T is [JT] cM (JT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CJ	15		CT	25		JT	37		geneorder	jct	tcj

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene N</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and J is [CJ] cM (CJ)</li><li>The distance between genes C and N is [CN] cM (CN)</li><li>The distance between genes J and N is [JN] cM (JN)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CJ	5		CN	18		JN	22		geneorder	jcn	ncj

FIB_PLUS	<p>f205_389e</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene D</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene T</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and H is [DH] cM (DH)</li><li>The distance between genes D and T is [DT] cM (DT)</li><li>The distance between genes H and T is [HT] cM (HT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DH	17		DT	8		HT	10		geneorder	dth	htd
FIB_PLUS	<p>4031_4668</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene J</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene R</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and R is [JR] cM (JR)</li><li>The distance between genes J and T is [JT] cM (JT)</li><li>The distance between genes R and T is [RT] cM (RT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JR	36		JT	20		RT	25		geneorder	jtr	rtj
FIB_PLUS	<p>f753_fdab</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene X</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and M is [AM] cM (AM)</li><li>The distance between genes A and X is [AX] cM (AX)</li><li>The distance between genes M and X is [MX] cM (MX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AM	30		AX	8		MX	25		geneorder	axm	mxa
FIB_PLUS	<p>a818_1547</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene N</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene T</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and N is [AN] cM (AN)</li><li>The distance between genes A and T is [AT] cM (AT)</li><li>The distance between genes N and T is [NT] cM (NT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AN	8		AT	25		NT	32		geneorder	nat	tan
FIB_PLUS	<p>6672_c5c8</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene T</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene W</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">h</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and T is [HT] cM (HT)</li><li>The distance between genes H and W is [HW] cM (HW)</li><li>The distance between genes T and W is [TW] cM (TW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HT	14		HW	25		TW	35		geneorder	thw	wht

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and M is [EM] cM (EM)</li><li>The distance between genes E and Y is [EY] cM (EY)</li><li>The distance between genes M and Y is [MY] cM (MY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EM	15		EY	25		MY	37		geneorder	mey	yem

FIB_PLUS	<p>ab03_a830</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene H</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and E is [BE] cM (BE)</li><li>The distance between genes B and H is [BH] cM (BH)</li><li>The distance between genes E and H is [EH] cM (EH)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BE	26		BH	5		EH	22		geneorder	bhe	ehb
FIB_PLUS	<p>0c2f_61f6</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene N</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and N is [AN] cM (AN)</li><li>The distance between genes A and T is [AT] cM (AT)</li><li>The distance between genes N and T is [NT] cM (NT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AN	36		AT	15		NT	24		geneorder	atn	nta
FIB_PLUS	<p>b555_012c</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #6d5e00;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene T</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene Y</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse;
