Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and T is [HT] cM (HT)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>The distance between genes T and Y is [TY] cM (TY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HT	12		HY	29		TY	20		geneorder	hty	yth
FIB_PLUS	<p>faf6_8428</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene A</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #6d5e00;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene M</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and M is [AM] cM (AM)</li><li>The distance between genes A and P is [AP] cM (AP)</li><li>The distance between genes M and P is [MP] cM (MP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AM	10		AP	18		MP	25		geneorder	map	pam

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene A</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #6d5e00;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene P</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene T</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and P is [AP] cM (AP)</li><li>The distance between genes A and T is [AT] cM (AT)</li><li>The distance between genes P and T is [PT] cM (PT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AP	20		AT	22		PT	38		geneorder	pat	tap

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene B</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene E</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and B is [AB] cM (AB)</li><li>The distance between genes A and E is [AE] cM (AE)</li><li>The distance between genes B and E is [BE] cM (BE)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AB	15		AE	5		BE	11		geneorder	aeb	bea

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene K</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene X</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and K is [BK] cM (BK)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes K and X is [KX] cM (KX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BK	20		BX	25		KX	38		geneorder	kbx	xbk
FIB_PLUS	<p>e57a_d29a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene H</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and P is [HP] cM (HP)</li><li>The distance between genes H and X is [HX] cM (HX)</li><li>The distance between genes P and X is [PX] cM (PX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HP	10		HX	31		PX	24		geneorder	hpx	xph
FIB_PLUS	<p>f787_f9ca</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene D</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #6d5e00;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene F</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and F is [DF] cM (DF)</li><li>The distance between genes D and T is [DT] cM (DT)</li><li>The distance between genes F and T is [FT] cM (FT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DF	10		DT	18		FT	25		geneorder	fdt	tdf
FIB_PLUS	<p>808c_460d</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene J</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and J is [AJ] cM (AJ)</li><li>The distance between genes A and X is [AX] cM (AX)</li><li>The distance between genes J and X is [JX] cM (JX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AJ	14		AX	25		JX	34		geneorder	jax	xaj

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene F</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene M</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene R</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and M is [FM] cM (FM)</li><li>The distance between genes F and R is [FR] cM (FR)</li><li>The distance between genes M and R is [MR] cM (MR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FM	6		FR	29		MR	25		geneorder	fmr	rmf

A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene H</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene R</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene W</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and R is [HR] cM (HR)</li><li>The distance between genes H and W is [HW] cM (HW)</li><li>The distance between genes R and W is [RW] cM (RW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HR	10		HW	30		RW	22		geneorder	hrw	wrh

Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes P and R is [PR] cM (PR)</li><li>The distance between genes P and X is [PX] cM (PX)</li><li>The distance between genes R and X is [RX] cM (RX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	PR	5		PX	22		RX	25		geneorder	rpx	xpr
FIB_PLUS	<p>ffef_25cc</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene C</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #6d5e00;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6d5e00;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene F</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene R</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and F is [CF] cM (CF)</li><li>The distance between genes C and R is [CR] cM (CR)</li><li>The distance between genes F and R is [FR] cM (FR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CF	26		CR	5		FR	22		geneorder	crf	frc
FIB_PLUS	<p>afc8_6863</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene H</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene J</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene N</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and J is [HJ] cM (HJ)</li><li>The distance between genes H and N is [HN] cM (HN)</li><li>The distance between genes J and N is [JN] cM (JN)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HJ	34		HN	10		JN	25		geneorder	hnj	jnh
FIB_PLUS	<p>ccf1_6164</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene F</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene H</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and F is [BF] cM (BF)</li><li>The distance between genes B and H is [BH] cM (BH)</li><li>The distance between genes F and H is [FH] cM (FH)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BF	20		BH	24		FH	38		geneorder	fbh	hbf
FIB_PLUS	<p>2040_d83a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene H</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene M</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene Y</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and M is [HM] cM (HM)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>The distance between genes M and Y is [MY] cM (MY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HM	10		HY	24		MY	32		geneorder	mhy	yhm

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene N</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and N is [DN] cM (DN)</li><li>The distance between genes D and X is [DX] cM (DX)</li><li>The distance between genes N and X is [NX] cM (NX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DN	9		DX	25		NX	33		geneorder	ndx	xdn

FIB_PLUS	<p>057c_a318</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and K is [BK] cM (BK)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes K and X is [KX] cM (KX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BK	22		BX	5		KX	18		geneorder	bxk	kxb
FIB_PLUS	<p>7a34_711c</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene T</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and K is [EK] cM (EK)</li><li>The distance between genes E and T is [ET] cM (ET)</li><li>The distance between genes K and T is [KT] cM (KT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EK	31		ET	8		KT	25		geneorder	etk	kte
FIB_PLUS	<p>66b2_14b2</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene D</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and D is [AD] cM (AD)</li><li>The distance between genes A and M is [AM] cM (AM)</li><li>The distance between genes D and M is [DM] cM (DM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AD	8		AM	10		DM	17		geneorder	dam	mad
FIB_PLUS	<p>e708_7fc5</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene D</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene F</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and D is [CD] cM (CD)</li><li>The distance between genes C and F is [CF] cM (CF)</li><li>The distance between genes D and F is [DF] cM (DF)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CD	37		CF	16		DF	25		geneorder	cfd	dfc
FIB_PLUS	<p>e0be_80ad</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene J</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and J is [AJ] cM (AJ)</li><li>The distance between genes A and K is [AK] cM (AK)</li><li>The distance between genes J and K is [JK] cM (JK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AJ	10		AK	24		JK	32		geneorder	jak	kaj

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene M</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene W</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and M is [CM] cM (CM)</li><li>The distance between genes C and W is [CW] cM (CW)</li><li>The distance between genes M and W is [MW] cM (MW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CM	15		CW	22		MW	31		geneorder	mcw	wcm

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene D</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene W</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and D is [AD] cM (AD)</li><li>The distance between genes A and W is [AW] cM (AW)</li><li>The distance between genes D and W is [DW] cM (DW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AD	5		AW	28		DW	24		geneorder	adw	wda
FIB_PLUS	<p>1dd3_3ec9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene F</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene J</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and F is [BF] cM (BF)</li><li>The distance between genes B and J is [BJ] cM (BJ)</li><li>The distance between genes F and J is [FJ] cM (FJ)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BF	36		BJ	12		FJ	25		geneorder	bjf	fjb
FIB_PLUS	<p>de06_57bb</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene F</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene J</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene W</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and J is [FJ] cM (FJ)</li><li>The distance between genes F and W is [FW] cM (FW)</li><li>The distance between genes J and W is [JW] cM (JW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FJ	30		FW	14		JW	20		geneorder	fwj	jwf
FIB_PLUS	<p>0000_c6e9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene P</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene R</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes P and R is [PR] cM (PR)</li><li>The distance between genes P and T is [PT] cM (PT)</li><li>The distance between genes R and T is [RT] cM (RT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	PR	7		PT	10		RT	16		geneorder	rpt	tpr

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene F</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene X</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">b</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large;
