Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and F is [BF] cM (BF)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes F and X is [FX] cM (FX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BF	34		BX	16		FX	25		geneorder	bxf	fxb
FIB_PLUS	<p>b856_a85e</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene K</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene M</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and K is [EK] cM (EK)</li><li>The distance between genes E and M is [EM] cM (EM)</li><li>The distance between genes K and M is [KM] cM (KM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EK	34		EM	14		KM	25		geneorder	emk	kme

FIB_PLUS	<p>507b_4780</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene E</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene K</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and K is [EK] cM (EK)</li><li>The distance between genes E and P is [EP] cM (EP)</li><li>The distance between genes K and P is [KP] cM (KP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EK	20		EP	24		KP	36		geneorder	kep	pek
FIB_PLUS	<p>4b9b_9e7b</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene W</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and J is [AJ] cM (AJ)</li><li>The distance between genes A and W is [AW] cM (AW)</li><li>The distance between genes J and W is [JW] cM (JW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AJ	5		AW	11		JW	15		geneorder	jaw	waj
FIB_PLUS	<p>70b8_2853</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene B</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and H is [BH] cM (BH)</li><li>The distance between genes B and K is [BK] cM (BK)</li><li>The distance between genes H and K is [HK] cM (HK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BH	5		BK	18		HK	14		geneorder	bhk	khb
FIB_PLUS	<p>5e2c_c499</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene J</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene K</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and J is [AJ] cM (AJ)</li><li>The distance between genes A and K is [AK] cM (AK)</li><li>The distance between genes J and K is [JK] cM (JK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AJ	21		AK	10		JK	12		geneorder	akj	jka

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene C</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene R</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and C is [AC] cM (AC)</li><li>The distance between genes A and R is [AR] cM (AR)</li><li>The distance between genes C and R is [CR] cM (CR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AC	21		AR	37		CR	25		geneorder	acr	rca

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene F</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene M</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and J is [FJ] cM (FJ)</li><li>The distance between genes F and M is [FM] cM (FM)</li><li>The distance between genes J and M is [JM] cM (JM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FJ	37		FM	21		JM	25		geneorder	fmj	jmf

FIB_PLUS	<p>5814_c97b</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene C</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene M</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and M is [CM] cM (CM)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>The distance between genes M and X is [MX] cM (MX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CM	5		CX	18		MX	14		geneorder	cmx	xmc
FIB_PLUS	<p>a6d1_4ec8</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and T is [CT] cM (CT)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>The distance between genes T and X is [TX] cM (TX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CT	14		CX	33		TX	25		geneorder	ctx	xtc
FIB_PLUS	<p>d880_5638</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene D</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene W</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and D is [BD] cM (BD)</li><li>The distance between genes B and W is [BW] cM (BW)</li><li>The distance between genes D and W is [DW] cM (DW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BD	5		BW	12		DW	16		geneorder	dbw	wbd
FIB_PLUS	<p>3686_5ced</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene M</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006a77;">Gene X</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006a77;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006a77;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene Y</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">m</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes M and X is [MX] cM (MX)</li><li>The distance between genes M and Y is [MY] cM (MY)</li><li>The distance between genes X and Y is [XY] cM (XY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	MX	5		MY	29		XY	25		geneorder	mxy	yxm
FIB_PLUS	<p>e43d_2147</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene N</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene Y</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and N is [JN] cM (JN)</li><li>The distance between genes J and Y is [JY] cM (JY)</li><li>The distance between genes N and Y is [NY] cM (NY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JN	4		JY	25		NY	28		geneorder	njy	yjn

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene J</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and J is [CJ] cM (CJ)</li><li>The distance between genes C and M is [CM] cM (CM)</li><li>The distance between genes J and M is [JM] cM (JM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CJ	30		CM	7		JM	25		geneorder	cmj	jmc

FIB_PLUS	<p>8d78_676d</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene C</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene W</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #9b344c;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9b344c;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and C is [AC] cM (AC)</li><li>The distance between genes A and W is [AW] cM (AW)</li><li>The distance between genes C and W is [CW] cM (CW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AC	11		AW	25		CW	31		geneorder	caw	wac
FIB_PLUS	<p>db4c_4878</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene M</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene N</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes M and N is [MN] cM (MN)</li><li>The distance between genes M and X is [MX] cM (MX)</li><li>The distance between genes N and X is [NX] cM (NX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	MN	20		MX	25		NX	39		geneorder	nmx	xmn
FIB_PLUS	<p>e877_c7ea</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene F</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #556707;">Gene R</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #556707;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #556707;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene Y</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and R is [FR] cM (FR)</li><li>The distance between genes F and Y is [FY] cM (FY)</li><li>The distance between genes R and Y is [RY] cM (RY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FR	24		FY	10		RY	15		geneorder	fyr	ryf
FIB_PLUS	<p>6232_391f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene T</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene W</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #306095;">Gene Y</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #306095;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #306095;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes T and W is [TW] cM (TW)</li><li>The distance between genes T and Y is [TY] cM (TY)</li><li>The distance between genes W and Y is [WY] cM (WY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	TW	31		TY	7		WY	25		geneorder	tyw	wyt
FIB_PLUS	<p>60a7_f06a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene C</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #7e5500;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7e5500;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene E</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #8e3b71;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8e3b71;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">c</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #266c51;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and E is [CE] cM (CE)</li><li>The distance between genes C and M is [CM] cM (CM)</li><li>The distance between genes E and M is [EM] cM (EM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CE	32		CM	11		EM	25		geneorder	cme	emc

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #84379a;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #84379a;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and E is [AE] cM (AE)</li><li>The distance between genes A and X is [AX] cM (AX)</li><li>The distance between genes E and X is [EX] cM (EX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AE	14		AX	25		EX	38		geneorder	eax	xae

FIB_PLUS	<p>97af_a3d9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene K</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene R</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and K is [CK] cM (CK)</li><li>The distance between genes C and R is [CR] cM (CR)</li><li>The distance between genes K and R is [KR] cM (KR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CK	16		CR	5		KR	12		geneorder	crk	krc
FIB_PLUS	<p>49c4_b99f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene E</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene R</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and E is [AE] cM (AE)</li><li>The distance between genes A and R is [AR] cM (AR)</li><li>The distance between genes E and R is [ER] cM (ER)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AE	16		AR	25		ER	38		geneorder	ear	rae
FIB_PLUS	<p>5c62_8cce</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #276f27;">Gene C</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #276f27;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #276f27;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene X</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #0e6689;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #0e6689;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and C is [BC] cM (BC)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BC	12		BX	25		CX	34		geneorder	cbx	xbc
FIB_PLUS	<p>9f2a_8f0f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene E</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene F</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and E is [BE] cM (BE)</li><li>The distance between genes B and F is [BF] cM (BF)</li><li>The distance between genes E and F is [EF] cM (EF)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BE	15		BF	24		EF	36		geneorder	ebf	fbe
FIB_PLUS	<p>932c_3670</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #894c1a;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #894c1a;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #266c51;">Gene F</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #266c51;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #266c51;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene K</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #6843b3;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #6843b3;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and F is [CF] cM (CF)</li><li>The distance between genes C and K is [CK] cM (CK)</li><li>The distance between genes F and K is [FK] cM (FK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CF	7		CK	25		FK	31		geneorder	fck	kcf
FIB_PLUS	<p>4a3c_43b9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #983e29;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #983e29;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006c66;">Gene H</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006c66;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006c66;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene K</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #3e52bf;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #3e52bf;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse;
